lines: Keep the ad framed as an advertising concept: the on-screen creator speaks as a paid actor demonstrating the product, never as a verified buyer, and never invents a personal purchase story or a lived-through result.
Forbid first-person purchase language such as owning, buying or having personally tried the product in real life; the presenter narrates what the product does, not what they experienced.
If a founder or origin angle is requested, voice it in the third person about the maker; never let the synthetic presenter assert that they personally founded or created the product.
Do not state or imply guaranteed outcomes; describe what the product does, not what it will certainly do for the viewer.
Surface no fabricated review, star rating, follower count or sales figure as if it were genuine social proof.
Leave out any endorsement, award, ranking or expert seal that has not been supplied as an approved asset.
Keep every spoken and written claim inside the boundaries of the brief; add no benefit, statistic or comparison that the brief did not provide.
Show no other brand's trademark, packaging or logo, and imitate no competitor's look to suggest a comparison the brief did not authorise.
Preserve the product's true geometry, proportions and colour across every cut; permit no melting, bending, extra parts or artefact along its silhouette.
Keep the product's real label, wordmark and packaging text intact and legible; invent no copy, badge or logo that is not already on the physical product.
Hold the creator's face and body consistent from shot to shot; allow no drift of identity, no morphing of features and no swapping of the person mid-clip.
Render hands, fingers and limbs anatomically correct throughout, with no fusing, duplication or missing digits.
Suppress warping, ghosting, flicker and other generation defects; the frame should read as a clean phone capture, not a distorted render.
Compose for a full-bleed 9:16 vertical frame and keep the product, the face and any burned-in words clear of the right-edge action rail and the bottom caption strip.
Favour a raw, handheld, off-the-cuff feel over a polished commercial look; the clip should pass as something filmed on a phone in one take.
Leave the top and lower thirds free of critical subject matter so the app's own overlays never cover the payoff.
Compose for a 9:16 vertical frame and keep the subject away from the lower band where the Reels caption, audio tag and profile row sit.
Keep motion smooth and the subject centred enough to survive the platform's slight crop of the top and bottom edges.
Aim for a bright, clean, native-to-feed look while still reading as creator-shot rather than studio-produced.
Make no medical, dermatological or 'clinically proven' promise, and stage no doctor, nurse or lab setting that implies clinical authority.
Keep skin real: show natural texture, pores and tone, and apply no smoothing, airbrushing or beauty-filter retouch to the creator's face.
Stage no exaggerated or overnight before-and-after that a real routine could not produce.
Promise no returns, income or profit, and imply no path to guaranteed wealth or risk-free earning.
Reproduce no real bank, card network or brokerage logo; render any on-screen account, balance or transaction as abstract placeholder blocks with no readable figures.
Present no fabricated account balance, payout screenshot or portfolio result as if it belonged to a real user.
Make no claim to cure, treat, diagnose or prevent any condition, and offer no dosage, symptom-relief or medical-outcome promise.
Depict no clinical, hospital or pharmacy setting and no white-coat figure that would imply professional medical endorsement.
Show no unsafe, extreme or unsupervised use of the product, and imply no result that a healthy routine could not deliver.
Render every on-screen interface as abstract cards, bars and blocks with no legible invented copy; show no real screenshot text that was not supplied as an asset.
Fabricate no metric, notification, chart or streak that overstates what the app actually does.
Display no competitor app icon, store badge or third-party brand mark inside the interface.
Make no nutritional, weight-loss or health-benefit claim beyond what the brief supplies, and imply no dietary result the product cannot support.
Keep the food true to its real form and colour; stage no impossible portion, gloss or transformation that misrepresents the actual item.
Invent no rating, award or 'chef-approved' seal, and show no rival restaurant or grocery brand mark.
Keep the garment true to its real cut, fabric, drape and colour; fabricate no pattern, logo or detail that the physical item does not have.
Reproduce no other label's monogram or trademark, and stage no lookalike that borrows a rival brand's identity.
Imply no body-transformation, slimming or 'instant fit' result that the clothing itself could not produce.
